title: WASSCE 2004 - Question 1
content:
All your burette readings (initial and final), as well as the size of your pipette, must be recorded but no account of experimental procedure is required.

A is a solution of HCl containing 5.0 g dmвЃ»Ві.
B is a solution of impure KOH containing 6.50 g dmвЃ»Ві.

(a) Put A into the burette and titrate it against 20.0 cmВі or 25.0 cmВі portions of B using methyl orange as indicator.

The equation for the reaction is:
HCl(aq) + KOH(aq) в†’ KCl(aq) + Hв‚‚O(l)

(b) From your results and the information provided above, calculate the:
(i) concentration of A in mol dmвЃ»Ві
(ii) concentration of B in mol dmвЃ»Ві
(iii) percentage purity of KOH in B

[H = 1; Cl = 35.5; K = 39.0; O = 16.0]

title: WASSCE 2003 - Question 1
content:
All your burette readings (initial and final), as well as the size of your pipette, must be recorded.

A is a solution of HCl containing 7.30 g dmвЃ»Ві.
B is a solution of Xв‚‚COв‚ѓ containing 10.6 g dmвЃ»Ві.

(a) Put A into the burette and titrate it against 20.0 cmВі or 25.0 cmВі portions of B using methyl orange as indicator.

The equation for the reaction is:
Xв‚‚COв‚ѓ + 2HCl в†’ 2XCl + Hв‚‚O + COв‚‚

(b) Calculate the:
(i) concentration of A in mol dmвЃ»Ві
(ii) concentration of B in mol dmвЃ»Ві
(iii) molar mass of Xв‚‚COв‚ѓ
(iv) relative atomic mass of X

[H = 1; C = 12; O = 16; Cl = 35.5]

title: WASSCE 2002 - Question 1
content:
All your burette readings (initial and final), as well as the size of your pipette, must be recorded.

A is 0.125 mol dmвЃ»Ві Hв‚‚SOв‚„ solution.
B is an aqueous solution containing x g dmвЃ»Ві of KOH.

(a) Put A into the burette and titrate it against 20.0 cmВі or 25.0 cmВі portions of B using methyl orange as indicator.

The equation for the reaction is:
Hв‚‚SOв‚„(aq) + 2KOH(aq) в†’ Kв‚‚SOв‚„(aq) + 2Hв‚‚O(l)

(b) Calculate the:
(i) average volume of A used
(ii) concentration of B in mol dmвЃ»Ві
(iii) concentration of B in g dmвЃ»Ві
(iv) value of x

[H = 1; O = 16; K = 39; S = 32]

title: WASSCE 2007 - Question 1
content:
All your burette readings (initial and final) as well as the size of your pipette, must be recorded.

A is a solution of HCl.
B is a solution containing 3.80 g of Naв‚‚COв‚ѓ per 500 cmВі of solution.

(a) Put A into the burette and titrate it against 25.0 cmВі portions of B using methyl orange as indicator. Repeat the titration to obtain consistent titres.

The equation for the reaction is:
Naв‚‚COв‚ѓ(aq) + 2HCl(aq) в†’ 2NaCl(aq) + Hв‚‚O(l) + COв‚‚(g)

(b) Calculate the:
(i) concentration of B in mol dmвЃ»Ві
(ii) concentration of A in mol dmвЃ»Ві

[H = 1; C = 12; O = 16; Na = 23; Cl = 35.5]

title: WASSCE 2009 - Question 1
content:
Burette readings (initial and final) must be given to two decimal places. Volume of pipette used must also be recorded.

A is a solution of HCl.
B is a solution of Naв‚‚COв‚ѓВ·XHв‚‚O containing 13.50 g per dmВі.

(a) Put A into the burette and titrate it against 25.0 cmВі portions of B using methyl orange as indicator. Repeat the titration to obtain consistent titres.

The equation for the reaction is:
Naв‚‚COв‚ѓ + 2HCl в†’ 2NaCl + Hв‚‚O + COв‚‚

(b) Calculate the:
(i) concentration of B in mol dmвЃ»Ві
(ii) molar mass of Naв‚‚COв‚ѓВ·XHв‚‚O
(iii) value of X

[H = 1; C = 12; O = 16; Na = 23; Cl = 35.5]

title: WASSCE 2011 - Question 1
content:
Burette readings (initial and final) must be given to two decimal places.

A is a 0.100 mol dmвЃ»Ві solution of an acid Hв‚‚Y.
B is a solution of KOH containing 2.8 g per 500 cmВі.

(a) Put A into the burette and titrate it against 20.0 cmВі or 25.0 cmВі portions of B using methyl orange as indicator. Repeat the titration to obtain consistent titres.

(b) Calculate the:
(i) number of moles of acid in the average titre
(ii) number of moles of KOH in the volume of B pipetted
(iii) mole ratio of acid to base in the reaction

[H = 1.00; O = 16.0; K = 39.0]
